content: Language learning's formative reflection practice (the common educational meaning of reflexion) can be repurposed as a structured scaffold for LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where the incremental feedback cycles that help human learners refine vocabulary and argumentative writing map directly to how LLMs can iteratively correct their reasoning errors. Just as high school students refine drafts by reflecting on mismatches between their intended meaning and their written output, LLMs can use this same graded reflective framework to contrast inconsistent solution perspectives and reduce logical gaps.
